message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Morgan's in the Desert (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 552 reviews; TripAdvisor 536 reviews; Restaurantji 623 reviews — aggregate roughly 1,500-2,000 across platforms
- Sentiment: Mostly positive but mixed — strong on ambiance/setting, more variable on food and service consistency
- Longevity: Resort opened 1926; restaurant has operated as the resort's signature dining venue for decades. Exact opening year of current Morgan's concept [UNVERIFIED] — sources cite the resort's 1926 origins but the Morgan's branded concept is more recent
- Credentials: ["TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice award","OpenTable Diners' Choice (2024 cited)","Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence (historical, [UNVERIFIED] current year)","Palm Springs Life feature coverage ('Hollywood A-List Reputation')","Previously led by 3x James Beard Award-winning chef Jimmy Schmidt (now departed per chef's own site; current exec chef [UNVERIFIED])","Hosted James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour dinners (historical)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably in 'best fine dining La Quinta', 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', and 'La Quinta Resort dining' queries. Less dominant in broader 'date night Rancho Mirage' or 'romantic Palm Desert' queries where Wally's Desert Turtle, The Edge Steakhouse, and Catalan tend to lead. Frequently named as 'undisputed anchor of fine dining in La Quinta' in AI-generated summaries.
- Site structured data: Two parallel web presences: laquintaresort.com/dining/morgans-in-the-desert/ (Waldorf Astoria-managed) and standalone morgansinthedesert.com (intermittently returning 500 errors during fetch). Resort page carries hours, address, phone, menu categories in readable HTML but no detectable schema.org Restaurant/Menu markup. Heavy reliance on Hilton/Waldorf parent CMS — agent-readable basics present, but no LocalBusiness JSON-LD, no machine-readable menu, no openingHoursSpecification block visible.
- Visibility gap score: 5
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"A Date With History at Morgan’s in the Desert in La Quinta | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/a-date-with-history-at-morgans-in-the-desert-in-la-quinta/"},{"title":"Morgans In The Desert - La Quinta American Resaurants and Bars","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/morgans-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"Morgan's in the desert Menu La Quinta Palm Springs CA | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/menu/morgans-desert-la-quinta-ca-92253_35ps091113-03.html"},{"title":"History of La Quinta Resort & Club","url":"https://laquintaresort.com/our-story/"},{"title":"Morgan's in the Desert | La Quinta Resort & Club Dining","url":"https://www.laquintaresort.com/dining/morgans-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"Morgan's in the desert | Villas at La Quinta Resort","url":"http://www.villasatlaquintaresort.com/dining/morgans.aspx"},{"title":"Morgan's in the Desert | davidlansing.com","url":"https://davidlansing.com/morgans-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"Morgan's in the Desert - La Quinta Resort & Club","url":"https://laquintaresort.com/restaurant/morgans-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"La Quinta Resort & Club - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/La_Quinta_Resort_&_Club"},{"title":"Menu for Morgan’s in the desert in La Quinta, CA | Sirved","url":"https://www.sirved.com/restaurant/la_quinta-california-usa/morgans-in-the-desert/581936/menus"}]

Based on the search results, here's the information about Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort:

Morgan's in the Desert was founded in 1926 by Walter H. Morgan, and originally built as The Dining Room in 1927. More specifically, Walter H. Morgan opened the La Quinta Hotel on December 29, 1926.

The restaurant has a rich history as Hollywood stars like Mary Pickford and Frank Capra often mingled there in its early days. It has been welcoming guests for intimate meals since 1926 and remains steeped in history and freshly reimagined, with a recently refreshed setting from bar to dining room that blends timeless charm with a renewed sense of style.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "Jimmy Schmidt James Beard Award winner chef Morgan's in the Desert"

Links: [{"title":"Chef Jimmy Schmidt leaves Morgans in the Desert – One Mad Chef","url":"http://jimmyschmidt.com/chef-jimmy-schmidt-leaves-morgans/"},{"title":"Highest Compliments to the Chef - James Beard awarded Jimmy Schmidt - Review of Morgan's In The Desert, La Quinta, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32584-d1987576-r99805863-Morgan_s_In_The_Desert-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"One Mad Chef","url":"http://jimmyschmidt.com/"},{"title":"Biography – One Mad Chef","url":"http://jimmyschmidt.com/biography/"},{"title":"Chef Jimmy Schmidt, Wine Portfolio, Eat, Drink, Travel","url":"http://www.wineportfolio.com/sectionRecipes-Chef-Jimmy-Schmidt-Bio.html"},{"title":"Morgan's in the desert | La Quinta, CA","url":"https://morgansinthedesert.com/?exam=132-S-709.2.html"},{"title":"Show 96, November 8, 2014: Chef Jimmy Schmidt and Morgan’s in the Desert - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/media/podcasts/show-96-november-8-2014-chef-jimmy-schmidt-morgans-desert/"},{"title":"Jimmy Schmidt and Morgan’s in the Desert – La Quinta, CA","url":"https://greatchefs.com/chef/jimmy-schmidt-videos-of-cooking-recipes/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Jimmy Schmidt, the James Beard Award-winning chef and Morgan's in the Desert:

Jimmy Schmidt is a three-time James Beard awarded chef who was significantly associated with Morgan's in the Desert. For seven years as the Executive Chef of Morgan's, he achieved numerous awards at Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort, including 2016 USA Today's Top Farm to Table Restaurant.

Schmidt is a pioneer in America's culinary movement towards sustainable cooking and farm-to-table dining, and founded Chefs Collaborative in 1991, the nation's leading nonprofit chefs organization devoted to fostering a sustainable food system.

He garnered national acclaim when he opened Rattlesnake Club restaurants in Denver and in Detroit, which now celebrates almost 30 years as an award-winning Midwest dining destination. Jimmy Schmidt left Morgan's in the Desert to pursue new projects with Lucky's Wagyu Beef BBQ and SmartCookie Popups, which were set to open in late 2018.

Based on the search results, I found information about Jimmy Schmidt and the James Beard Award for Best Chef Midwest. However, there's a discrepancy in the year:

In 1991, Jimmy Schmidt of The Rattlesnake Club in Detroit, MI won the Best Chef: Midwest James Beard Award. However, other sources indicate Jimmy Schmidt won the James Beard Best Chef Midwest award in 1993.

The sources also confirm that Jimmy Schmidt has received three prestigious James Beard Awards for Best Chef Midwest throughout his career. Additional context shows that the first James Beard Foundation Awards were presented on May 6, 1991, aboard the luxury liner M/S New Yorker, in a ceremony hosted by George Plimpton.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Morgan's in the Desert and its Wine Spectator Award of Excellence:

Morgan's in the Desert received the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence in 2016. The restaurant is located inside the La Quinta Resort & Club.

The restaurant is described as a Coachella Valley connoisseur's dream destination with locally selected ingredients promoting the bounty of the desert. Morgan's in the desert showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, sourcing the region's top products and crafting them with traditional cooking methods.

Links: [{"title":"Morgan's in the desert | La Quinta, CA","url":"https://morgansinthedesert.com/"},{"title":"Palm Springs Farm to Table Restaurants: 10Best Restaurant Reviews","url":"https://www.10best.com/destinations/california/palm-springs/la-quinta/restaurants/morgans-in-the-desert-1/"},{"title":"Morgan's in the Desert | davidlansing.com","url":"https://davidlansing.com/morgans-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"La Quinta Resort & Club introduces New Menu at Morgan's","url":"https://www.fb101.com/chefs-on-the-move-la-quinta-resort-club-introduces-michelin-caliber-chef-new-seasonal-menu-at-morgans-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"MORGAN'S IN THE DESERT, La Quinta - Menu, Prices, Restaurant Reviews & Reservations - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32584-d1987576-Reviews-Morgan_s_In_The_Desert-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Morgan's in the desert | Villas at La Quinta Resort","url":"http://www.villasatlaquintaresort.com/dining/morgans.aspx"},{"title":"MORGAN’S IN THE DESERT - Updated May 2026 - 612 Photos & 552 Reviews - 49-499 Eisenhower Dr, La Quinta, California - American - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/morgans-in-the-desert-la-quinta"},{"title":"Blog - La Quinta Resort & Club","url":"https://laquintaresort.com/blog/"},{"title":"Morgan's in the Desert Restaurant - La Quinta, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/morgans-in-the-desert-la-quinta"},{"title":"Chef Jimmy Schmidt leaves Morgans in the Desert – One Mad Chef","url":"http://jimmyschmidt.com/chef-jimmy-schmidt-leaves-morgans/"}]

Based on the web search results, there are conflicting sources about the current executive chef at Morgan's in the Desert:

The restaurant's official website indicates that Morgan's in the Desert is led by three-time James Beard Award Winning chef Jimmy Schmidt, and Chef Jimmy Schmidt is responsible for creating the farm-to-table restaurant and creates seasonal menus incorporating fresh produce from the Coachella Valley.

However, a 2021 announcement indicated that Chef Jos van Hengel took over as Chef De Cuisine at Morgan's in the Desert. Additionally, a more recent source from the La Quinta Resort blog mentions Executive Chef Andrew Cooper in relation to La Quinta Resort's vibrant menu, though this reference appears to be for the broader resort rather than specifically Morgan's in the Desert.

The most current information from the official Morgan's in the Desert website suggests Jimmy Schmidt remains the primary chef associated with the restaurant, though the relationship between these various chefs' roles requires clarification to determine the exact current leadership structure for 2025-2026.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Morgan's in the Desert and the Michelin Guide:

Morgan's in the Desert is La Quinta's signature dining venue showcasing contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley. As the flagship restaurant of the legendary desert hideaway, this elegant room recalls the look of the resort when Walter H. Morgan opened it in 1926.

Regarding the Michelin Guide specifically: La Quinta doesn't have a Michelin-starred restaurant in the strictly decorated sense – the Michelin Guide has historically given California's desert communities a wider berth than, say, Los Angeles or San Francisco. However, the search results note that Chef Jos van Hengel refined his culinary prowess in some of the world's top kitchens, including Michelin-starred Parkheuvel in Rotterdam, Netherlands and Alinea in Chicago, Illinois.

While Morgan's in the Desert doesn't currently hold a Michelin star, it is recognized as the fine dining anchor of La Quinta and features high-caliber culinary talent with Michelin-level experience.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Jimmy Schmidt's James Beard Awards:

Jimmy Schmidt received 3 James Beard Awards: "Great American Chef" in 1987, "Best Chef Midwest" in 1993, and "Who's Who of Cooking in America" in 1984.

So while he did win James Beard Awards in the years you mentioned (1984 and 1993), his three awards span from 1984 to 1993, with the middle award coming in 1987 rather than 1991.

# Jimmy Schmidt's Departure from Morgan's in the Desert

**When:** Jimmy Schmidt left Morgan's in the Desert in November 2016, after serving as Executive Chef for seven years.

**His Statement:** Schmidt explained his departure by noting he was "following his culinary imagination to new projects with Lucky's Wagyu Beef BBQ and SmartCookie Popups, opening late 2018." He emphasized continuity by noting that former chef Brian Recor and other team members were joining him in these new ventures.

The announcement highlights his accomplishments at Morgan's, including securing USA Today's Top Farm to Table Restaurant award and Open Table's Best for Foodies recognition in 2016, before moving on to pursue fresh culinary directions.

Based on the search results, I found several relevant connections between Morgan's in the Desert and the James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour:

Morgan's in the Desert, located at La Quinta Resort, hosted annual James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour Dinners, and was recognized as USA Today's Top Farm to Table Restaurant and Open Table's Best for Foodies.

The restaurant was founded as part of the Waldorf Astoria at La Quinta concept.

Additionally, Chef Jimmy Schmidt, a James Beard Foundation Award winner associated with Morgan's in the Desert in La Quinta, California, was featured as a guest chef at various James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour dinners held in different cities.

The Celebrity Chef Tour started in 2004 with the intention of bringing the James Beard House experience on the road, created to imitate an intimate dinner party where guests can interact with chefs while enjoying multiple courses paired with wines.

content.text: I have enough to finalize. Let me note: Schmidt left in Nov 2016, but the resort's standalone morgansinthedesert.com still lists him (intermittently 500), while the official Waldorf-managed page does not name a chef. The Wikipedia primary lists 1993 Best Chef Midwest. Claim of "3x James Beard Award-winning" appears supported by his own bio (Great American Chef 1987, Who's Who 1984, Best Chef Midwest 1993).

confirms: Resort opened 1926 — Walter H. Morgan opened the La Quinta Hotel December 29, 1926 (confirmed via Palm Springs Life and La Quinta Resort history page)
Jimmy Schmidt won the James Beard Best Chef: Midwest award in 1993 at The Rattlesnake Club, Detroit (confirmed via Wikipedia 1990s JBF Awards listing)
Jimmy Schmidt is widely cited as a three-time James Beard honoree (Who's Who 1984, Great American Chef 1987, Best Chef Midwest 1993) per his own biography
Jimmy Schmidt has departed Morgan's — left November 2016 after seven years as Executive Chef (confirmed via his own site, jimmyschmidt.com)
Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (Morgan's received it in 2016, per Coachella Valley Weekly / La Quinta Resort reporting)
Hosted James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour dinners historically (referenced in multiple sources tied to Schmidt era)
Palm Springs Life feature coverage exists (dedicated 'A Date With History' feature confirmed)
contradicts: The morgansinthedesert.com standalone site still presents Jimmy Schmidt as the leading chef despite his Nov 2016 departure — this is stale content, not a current credential. Any AI-generated claim that Schmidt is the current chef would be inaccurate. The official Waldorf-managed laquintaresort.com dining page lists no chef name at all; a 2021 announcement named Jos van Hengel as Chef de Cuisine, and a more recent La Quinta Resort source mentions Executive Chef Andrew Cooper at resort level — current Morgan's-specific leadership for 2025-2026 remains unstated on the official Waldorf page.
flagged_as_unverified: Exact opening year of the current 'Morgan's in the Desert' branded concept — sources conflate the 1926 resort opening with the restaurant's branding; the named 'Morgan's' concept (after Walter H. Morgan) appears to be a later rebrand of 'The Dining Room' but no primary source pinned the rebrand year

OpenTable Diners' Choice 2024 specifically — could not independently confirm a 2024 OpenTable Diners' Choice award; OpenTable presence is confirmed but the 2024 award year citation is not verified from a primary source

Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence — the cited award level appears to be the standard 'Award of Excellence' (2016), not 'Best of Award of Excellence' which is a higher tier; current-year status is not verifiable
TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice award — referenced in search summaries but not directly verified on the TripAdvisor listing with a year
Current executive chef identity — official Waldorf page lists no chef; conflicting third-party references (Jos van Hengel 2021, Andrew Cooper resort-level)
Review counts (Yelp 552, TripAdvisor 536, Restaurantji 623) — Yelp 552 matches a search-result snippet; others not independently confirmed in this pass
input.notes: Schmidt's JBF credentials and historical role at Morgan's are well-supported, but he has been gone since Nov 2016. The biggest credentials-and-history risk: Morgan's standalone domain (morgansinthedesert.com) still markets Schmidt as the chef nearly a decade after he left, while the parent Waldorf page lists no chef. Any AI surface that scrapes the standalone site will produce stale, refutable claims. The 1926 resort founding is solid; the 'Morgan's' concept branding year remains unclear. Wine Spectator award is confirmed at Award of Excellence level for 2016 — the claim's 'Best of' tier wording may be an upgrade error.
